page: UpCloud PREMIUM-2xCPU-16GB is a KVM-based virtual server configured with 2 shared vCPUs mapped to 2 physical cores of an AMD EPYC 9575F processor running at 2.0 GHz. The system includes 16.0 GB of system memory, yielding 8.0 GB per core. Storage is provided via a bundled 150 GB local SSD. The CPU architecture is x86_64, featuring an L3 cache of 16 MB and advanced instruction sets including AVX-512 and cryptographic acceleration.

Benchmark results show strong single-core capabilities alongside moderate multi-core and memory performance. The server achieves top-tier performance in stress-ng single-core CPU tests and single-threaded compression tasks. However, multi-threaded decompression and overall PassMark CPU and memory scores fall into the lower tiers. Memory bandwidth is moderate, reaching 423.32 GB/sec for cached small blocks and approximately 68.14 GB/sec for uncached large blocks. Static web serving throughput is average at 7.52 GB/sec, while database operations and Redis SET operations show lower-tier performance.

The shared CPU allocation introduces potential resource contention, which is reflected in the lower multi-threaded and database benchmark tiers. This makes the server less optimal for sustained heavy multi-threaded compute or high-throughput database operations. However, the high memory-to-vCPU ratio and strong single-core performance offer qualitative cost efficiency for memory-bound workloads that do not require continuous, dedicated CPU performance. Recommended workloads include memory-intensive caching, lightweight web serving, single-threaded processing tasks, and general-purpose development environments.
description: UpCloud PREMIUM-2xCPU-16GB is a KVM-based virtual server featuring 2 shared vCPUs on an AMD EPYC 9575F processor running at 2.0 GHz, paired with 16.0 GB of RAM and 150 GB of local SSD storage. Built on the x86_64 architecture, the processor supports AVX-512 and cryptographic acceleration. Benchmarks indicate top-tier single-core CPU performance and single-threaded compression speeds, though multi-threaded operations, memory scores, and database performance fall into lower tiers. The 1:8 memory-to-vCPU ratio provides qualitative cost efficiency for memory-heavy workloads that do not demand dedicated CPU resources. This server is appropriate for memory-intensive caching, lightweight web serving, and single-threaded processing tasks.
og_description: UpCloud PREMIUM-2xCPU-16GB is a KVM virtual server with 2 shared AMD EPYC vCPUs, 16 GB RAM, and a 150 GB SSD, delivering top-tier single-core performance but lower multi-threaded efficiency.
meta_description: UpCloud PREMIUM-2xCPU-16GB features 2 shared AMD EPYC vCPUs, 16 GB RAM, and a 150 GB SSD, offering strong single-core performance.
tagline: A memory-optimized virtual server with strong single-core performance and bundled SSD storage for memory-bound, non-dedicated CPU workloads.
bullet_points: 2 shared AMD EPYC 9575F vCPUs and 16.0 GB of system memory
Bundled 150 GB local SSD storage and 1 public IPv4 address
Top-tier single-core CPU and single-threaded compression performance
AVX-512 and cryptographic hardware acceleration support
Best suited for memory-intensive caching and lightweight web serving
